candidates.notes: CRITICAL FINDINGS: (1) Uniform contamination (s + alpha*mu_j) has ZERO effect on all metrics including Kendall tau -- confirmed rank-invariant as expected. (2) Structured contamination (s + alpha*|mu_j|*noise_i) and magnitude-proportional contamination (s + alpha*scale*noise_ij) both degrade performance monotonically with alpha. RepSim degrades faster than TRAK (35pp vs 9pp at alpha=1.0 on counterfact). (3) CRITICAL NEGATIVE: Contrastive correction (mean subtraction) does NOT recover performance from structured contamination. Corrected scores are identical to contaminated scores because noise_i is per-sample and mean subtraction only removes column-constant shifts. (4) This REFRAMES FM2: the original H11 hypothesis assumed contrastive correction could fix common influence contamination. But if contamination is sample-specific (non-uniform), mean subtraction is powerless. FM2 as formulated in the proposal (mean-subtraction as remedy) is fundamentally limited to uniform contamination patterns, which are already rank-invariant and thus undetectable. (5) Implication: FM2 is a THEORETICAL concept without practical measurement consequences -- uniform contamination doesn't affect any metric, and non-uniform contamination can't be fixed by mean subtraction.
